Do not edit GOAL.md.\n\n## Goal\n\ncome up with state of the art decentralised training algorithms  starting from here https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.15706 . use basilica for compute https://docs.basilica.ai/introduction\n\n## State\n\n# Quentin - Decentralized Training State\n\n## Research & Analysis\n- **Paper Analysis (SparseLoCo - arXiv:2508.15706):**\n    - Key Innovation: Combines **DiLoCo** (infrequent communication) with **TOP-k sparsification** and **Error Feedback (EF)**.\n    - Observation: Outer momentum can be approximated locally by an EF accumulator.\n    - Performance: Reaches 1-3% sparsity while outperforming full-precision DiLoCo.\n    - Mechanism: Replaces global outer momentum with a single local EF accumulator.\n    - Scalability: Shown to scale to 2B parameters and 32+ workers.\n- **Paper Analysis (Heterogeneous Low-Bandwidth Pre-Training - arXiv:2601.02360):**\n    - Key Innovation: Combines **SparseLoCo** with **Subspace Projection** for activation/gradient compression.\n    - Observation: Heterogeneous configurations (mixing compressed and uncompressed replicas) consistently outperform uniform compression.\n    - Mechanism: Selective compression based on interconnect bandwidth; handles embedding drift via projection back to subspace.\n    - Performance: Activation compression composes with SparseLoCo at modest cost; bias reduction from uncompressed replicas anchors aggregation.\n- **Basilica Analysis:**\n    - Provides on-demand compute (GPUs/CPUs) via Bittensor (The Bourse) or Datacenters (The Citadel).\n    - Supports containerized deployments and raw SSH rentals.\n    - Pay-per-minute via TAO credits.\n    - Ideal for heterogeneous, permissionless compute environments.\n\n## Proposed Strategy: \"Heterogeneous Basilica SparseLoCo\"\nTo make decentralized training real on Basilica, we adapt SparseLoCo for its specific constraints:\n1. **Heterogeneous Compression:** Use Subspace Projection (arXiv:2601.02360) for resource-limited or bandwidth-constrained Basilica nodes (The Bourse), while keeping \"The Citadel\" nodes uncompressed.\n2. **Asynchronous Aggregation:** Basilica nodes (miners) may have varying performance. We use an asynchronous version of SparseLoCo where the \"Parameter Server\" or \"Aggregator\" handles stale sparse updates with staleness compensation.\n3. **Verification of Work (SPoT):** Since Basilica miners are self-interested, we use Sparse Proof of Training (SPoT) to verify that the sparse pseudo-gradients are actually computed from the data using deterministic replay.\n4. **Dynamic Topology & Density:** Nodes may join/leave. The algorithm handles a dynamic $R$ (number of replicas) via the asynchronous aggregator. We also implement **Dynamic Density Adjustment** to scale communication overhead based on real-time network latency.\n\n## Implementation Progress\n- [x] Initial project structure defined.\n- [x] Print a summary to console\n        print(f\"[Citadel Dashboard] T+{elapsed:.1f}s | Version: {global_version} | Workers: {len(active_workers)}\")\n        for wid, reward in worker_rewards.items():\n            slashes = worker_slashes.get(wid, 0)\n            print(f\"  - {wid}: Rewards={reward}, Slashes={slashes}\")\n\napp = FastAPI()\n\nmodel = GlobalModel()\naggregator = BasilicaAggregator(model)\ntracker = ConvergenceTracker()\n\n@app.get(\"/metrics\")\nasync def get_metrics():\n    \"\"\"Returns real-time convergence metrics for the dashboard.\"\"\"\n    if not tracker.history:\n        return {\"error\": \"No data yet\"}\n    \n    latest = tracker.history[-1]\n    # Calculate throughput (updates/sec)\n    throughput = latest['version'] / latest['timestamp'] if latest['timestamp'] > 0 else 0\n    \n    return {\n        \"elapsed_seconds\": latest['timestamp'],\n        \"global_version\": latest['version'],\n        \"throughput_ups\": throughput,\n        \"active_workers\": latest['num_workers'],\n        \"total_rewards\": sum(latest['rewards'].values()),\n        \"total_slashes\": sum(latest['slashes'].values()),\n        \"worker_details\": [\n            {\n                \"worker_id\": wid,\n                \"reward\": latest['rewards'].get(wid, 0),\n                \"slashes\": latest['slashes'].get(wid, 0)\n            } for wid in latest['rewards'].keys()\n        ]\n    }\n\n@app.get(\"/weights\")\nasync def get_weights():\n    \"\"\"Returns the current global model weights.\"\"\"\n    weights = aggregator.get_global_weights()\n    # Convert tensors to lists for JSON serialization\n    return {\"weights\": [w.tolist() for w in weights], \"version\": aggregator.global_version}\n\n@app.post(\"/push_update\")\nasync def push_update(update_data: Dict[str, Any]):\n    \"\"\"\n    Receives sparse updates from workers.\n    Expects: worker_id, version, bits, indices, scales, verification_data (optional)\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Convert lists back to tensors\n        bits = [torch.tensor(b, dtype=torch.int8) for b in update_data['bits']]\n        indices = [torch.tensor(i, dtype=torch.long) for i in update_data['indices']]\n        scales = [torch.tensor(s, dtype=torch.float32) for s in update_data['scales']]\n        \n        verification_data = update_data.get('verification_data')\n        if verification_data:\n            # Reconstruct tensors in verification_data\n            verification_data['initial_weights'] = [torch.tensor(w) for w in verification_data['initial_weights']]\n            verification_data['data_shard'] = (\n                torch.tensor(verification_data['data_shard'][0]),\n                torch.tensor(verification_data['data_shard'][1])\n            )\n\n        success = aggregator.apply_sparse_update(\n            bits, \n            indices, \n            scales, \n            update_data['worker_id'], \n            update_data['version'],\n            verification_data=verification_data\n        )\n        \n        if not success:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail=\"Update rejected by verification/incentive logic\")\n            \n        # handles embedding drift via projection back to subspace.\n    - Performance: Activation compression composes with SparseLoCo at modest cost; bias reduction from uncompressed replicas anchors aggregation.\n- **Basilica Analysis:**\n    - Provides on-demand compute (GPUs/CPUs) via Bittensor (The Bourse) or Datacenters (The Citadel).\n    - Supports containerized deployments and raw SSH rentals.\n    - Pay-per-minute via TAO credits.\n    - Ideal for heterogeneous, permissionless compute environments.\n\n## Proposed Strategy: \"Heterogeneous Basilica SparseLoCo\"\nTo make decentralized training real on Basilica, we adapt SparseLoCo for its specific constraints:\n1. **Heterogeneous Compression:** Use Subspace Projection (arXiv:2601.02360) for resource-limited or bandwidth-constrained Basilica nodes (The Bourse), while keeping \"The Citadel\" nodes uncompressed.\n2. **Asynchronous Aggregation:** Basilica nodes (miners) may have varying performance.             if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in Use Basilica SDK to deploy\\n            max_retries = 3\\n            for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n                try:\\n                    deployment = self.client.deploy(\\n                        name=name,\\n                        source=worker_code,\\n                        gpu_count=1,\\n                        gpu_models=[\\\"A10G\\\"],\\n                        pip_packages=[\\\"torch\\\", \\\"numpy\\\", \\\"basilica-sdk\\\", \\\"requests\\\", \\\"uv\\\"],\\n                        env_vars={\\n                            \\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\": API_KEY,\\n                            \\\"CITADEL_URL\\\": self.config.get(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\", \\\"http://localhost:8000\\\"),\\n                            \\\"WORKER_ID\\\": name,\\n                            \\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\": str(is_compressed),\\n                            \\\"SPARSE_DENSITY\\\": \\\"0.03\\\" # Initial density\\n                        }\\n                    )\\n                    self.deployments.append(deployment)\\n                    print(f\\\"Worker {i} live at: {deployment.url}\\\")\\n                    break\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Attempt {attempt + 1} failed for {name}: {e}\\\")\\n                    if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                        print(f\\\"Failed to deploy {name} after {max_retries} attempts.\\\")\\n                    else:\\n                        time.sleep(5)\\n\\n    def _generate_worker_code(self, is_compressed: bool):\\n        # Convert tensors to lists for JSON\\n        serializable_update = {\\n            \\\"worker_id\\\": WORKER_ID,\\n            \\\"version\\\": self.version,\\n            \\\"bits\\\": [u['bits'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"indices\\\": [u['indices'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"scales\\\": [u['scale'].item() if u else 0.0 for u in update['updates']]\\n        }\\n        \\n        if verification_data:\\n            serializable_update[\\\"verification_data\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"initial_weights\\\": [w.tolist() for w in verification_data['initial_weights']],\\n                \\\"data_shard\\\": [verification_data['data_shard'][0].tolist(), verification_data['data_shard'][1].tolist()],\\n                \\\"h_steps\\\": verification_data['h_steps'],\\n                \\\"lr\\\": verification_data['lr'],\\n                \\\"seed\\\": verification_data['seed']\\n            }\\n        \\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.post(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/push_update\\\", json=serializable_update, timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on sync time\\n                    self.optimizer.adjust_density(sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # Read the required files to bundle them\\n        with open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            basilica_training_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            sparseloco_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            main_model_code = f.read()\\n\\n        return f\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nimport os\\nimport sys\\n\\n# Write bundled modules to disk so they can be imported\\nwith open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(basilica_training_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(sparseloco_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(main_model_code)})\\n\\nimport torch\\nimport torch.nn as nn\\nfrom basilica_training import HeterogeneousSparseLoCo\\nimport requests\\nimport json\\nimport time\\n\\n# Config from environment\\nAPI_KEY = os.getenv(\\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\")\\nCITADEL_URL = os.getenv(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\")\\nWORKER_ID = os.getenv(\\\"WORKER_ID\\\")\\nIS_COMPRESSED = os.getenv(\\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\") == \\\"True\\\"\\nSPARSE_DENSITY = float(os.getenv(\\\"SPARSE_DENSITY\\\", \\\"0.03\\\"))\\n\\nclass Worker:\\n    def __init__(self, model_class, is_compressed, density):\\n        self.model = model_class()\\n        self.optimizer = HeterogeneousSparseLoCo(\\n            self.model, \\n            is_compressed=is_compressed,\\n            density=density\\n        )\\n        self.version = 0\\n\\n    def push_update(self, update, verification_data=None):\\n        # Convert tensors to lists for JSON\\n        serializable_update = {\\n            \\\"worker_id\\\": WORKER_ID,\\n            \\\"version\\\": self.version,\\n            \\\"bits\\\": [u['bits'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"indices\\\": [u['indices'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"scales\\\": [u['scale'].item() if u else 0.0 for u in update['updates']]\\n        }\\n        \\n        if verification_data:\\n            serializable_update[\\\"verification_data\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"initial_weights\\\": [w.tolist() for w in verification_data['initial_weights']],\\n                \\\"data_shard\\\": [verification_data['data_shard'][0].tolist(), verification_data['data_shard'][1].tolist()],\\n                \\\"h_steps\\\": verification_data['h_steps'],\\n                \\\"lr\\\": verification_data['lr'],\\n                \\\"seed\\\": verification_data['seed']\\n            }\\n        \\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.post(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/push_update\\\", json=serializable_update, timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in decentralized setting\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            start_time = time.time()\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on sync time\\n                    self.optimizer.adjust_density(sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # 1. Update config with live Citadel URL\\n        self.config[\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\"] = citadel_url\\n        \\n        # 2. Launch workers\\n        self.launch_workers(num_workers=12) # Scaled to 12 heterogeneous workers\\n        \\n        # 3. Monitor and Aggregate (The Citadel/Aggregator logic)\\n        print(f\\\"Experiment running with Citadel at {citadel_url}. Monitoring workers...\\\")\\n        \\n        start_time = time.time()\\n        last_version = 0\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            try:\\n                # Query the new /metrics endpoint\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{self.config['CITADEL_URL']}/metrics\\\", timeout=10)\\n                if resp.status_code == 200:\\n                    data = resp.json()\\n                    if \\\"error\\\" in data:\\n                        print(f\\\"Waiting for data from Citadel...\\\")\\n                    else:\\n                        current_version = data['global_version']\\n                        throughput = data['throughput_ups']\\n                        active = data['active_workers']\\n                        print(f\\\"[Monitor] Version: {current_version} | UPS: {throughput:.2f} | Workers: {active}\\\")\\n                        \\n                        if current_version > last_version:\\n                            last_version = current_version\\n                else:\\n                    print(f\\\"Citadel Metrics Error: {resp.status_code}\\\")\\n                time.sleep(10)\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Monitoring error: {e}\\\")\\n                time.sleep(10)\\n        \\n    def cleanup(self):\\n        for d in self.deployments:\\n            try:\\n                self.client.delete(d.id)\\n            except:\\n                pass\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    from deploy_citadel import deploy_citadel\\n    \\n    # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in decentralized setting\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            start_time = time.time()\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            # For SPoT, we need to capture the state before local steps\\n            initial_weights = [p.data.clone().detach() for p in self.model.parameters()]\\n            seed = int(time.time() * 1000) % 100000\\n            torch.manual_seed(seed)\\n\\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                \\n                # Prepare verification data (SPoT)\\n                verification_data = {\\n                    \\\"initial_weights\\\": initial_weights,\\n                    \\\"data_shard\\\": (data, target),\\n                    \\\"h_steps\\\": 1,\\n                    \\\"lr\\\": 1e-4,\\n                    \\\"seed\\\": seed\\n                }\\n\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update, verification_data=verification_data)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    \\n                    # Synchronize and adjust density\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights, network_latency=sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Use Basilica SDK to deploy\\n            max_retries = 3\\n            for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n                try:\\n                    deployment = self.client.deploy(\\n                        name=name,\\n                        source=worker_code,\\n                        gpu_count=1,\\n                        gpu_models=[\\\"A10G\\\"],\\n                        pip_packages=[\\\"torch\\\", \\\"numpy\\\", \\\"basilica-sdk\\\", \\\"requests\\\", \\\"uv\\\"],\\n                        env_vars={\\n                            \\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\": API_KEY,\\n                            \\\"CITADEL_URL\\\": self.config.get(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\", \\\"http://localhost:8000\\\"),\\n                            \\\"WORKER_ID\\\": name,\\n                            \\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\": str(is_compressed),\\n                            \\\"SPARSE_DENSITY\\\": \\\"0.03\\\" # Initial density\\n                        }\\n                    )\\n                    self.deployments.append(deployment)\\n                    print(f\\\"Worker {i} live at: {deployment.url}\\\")\\n                    break\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Attempt {attempt + 1} failed for {name}: {e}\\\")\\n                    if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                        print(f\\\"Failed to deploy {name} after {max_retries} attempts.\\\")\\n                    else:\\n                        time.sleep(5)\\n\\n    def _generate_worker_code(self, is_compressed: bool):\\n        # Read the required files to bundle them\\n        with open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            basilica_training_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            sparseloco_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            main_model_code = f.read()\\n\\n        return f\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nimport os\\nimport sys\\n\\n# Write bundled modules to disk so they can be imported\\nwith open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(basilica_training_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(sparseloco_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(main_model_code)})\\n\\nimport torch\\nimport torch.nn as nn\\nfrom basilica_training import HeterogeneousSparseLoCo\\nimport requests\\nimport json\\nimport time\\n\\n# Config from environment\\nAPI_KEY = os.getenv(\\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\")\\nCITADEL_URL = os.getenv(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\")\\nWORKER_ID = os.getenv(\\\"WORKER_ID\\\")\\nIS_COMPRESSED = os.getenv(\\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\") == \\\"True\\\"\\nSPARSE_DENSITY = float(os.getenv(\\\"SPARSE_DENSITY\\\", \\\"0.03\\\"))\\n\\nclass Worker:\\n    def __init__(self, model_class, is_compressed, density):\\n        self.model = model_class()\\n        self.optimizer = HeterogeneousSparseLoCo(\\n            self.model, \\n            is_compressed=is_compressed,\\n            density=density\\n        )\\n        self.version = 0\\n\\n    def push_update(self, update, verification_data=None):\\n        # Convert tensors to lists for JSON\\n        serializable_update = {\\n            \\\"worker_id\\\": WORKER_ID,\\n            \\\"version\\\": self.version,\\n            \\\"bits\\\": [u['bits'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"indices\\\": [u['indices'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"scales\\\": [u['scale'].item() if u else 0.0 for u in update['updates']]\\n        }\\n        \\n        if verification_data:\\n            serializable_update[\\\"verification_data\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"initial_weights\\\": [w.tolist() for w in verification_data['initial_weights']],\\n                \\\"data_shard\\\": [verification_data['data_shard'][0].tolist(), verification_data['data_shard'][1].tolist()],\\n                \\\"h_steps\\\": verification_data['h_steps'],\\n                \\\"lr\\\": verification_data['lr'],\\n                \\\"seed\\\": verification_data['seed']\\n            }\\n        \\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.post(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/push_update\\\", json=serializable_update, timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in decentralized setting\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            start_time = time.time()\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on sync time\\n                    self.optimizer.adjust_density(sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # 1. Update config with live Citadel URL\\n        self.config[\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\"] = citadel_url\\n        \\n        # 2. Launch workers\\n        self.launch_workers(num_workers=12) # Scaled to 12 heterogeneous workers\\n        \\n        # 3. Monitor and Aggregate (The Citadel/Aggregator logic)\\n        print(f\\\"Experiment running with Citadel at {citadel_url}. Monitoring workers...\\\")\\n        \\n        start_time = time.time()\\n        last_version = 0\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            try:\\n                # Query the new /metrics endpoint\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{self.config['CITADEL_URL']}/metrics\\\", timeout=10)\\n                if resp.status_code == 200:\\n                    data = resp.json()\\n                    if \\\"error\\\" in data:\\n                        print(f\\\"Waiting for data from Citadel...\\\")\\n                    else:\\n                        current_version = data['global_version']\\n                        throughput = data['throughput_ups']\\n                        active = data['active_workers']\\n                        print(f\\\"[Monitor] Version: {current_version} | UPS: {throughput:.2f} | Workers: {active}\\\")\\n                        \\n                        if current_version > last_version:\\n                            last_version = current_version\\n                else:\\n                    print(f\\\"Citadel Metrics Error: {resp.status_code}\\\")\\n                time.sleep(10)\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Monitoring error: {e}\\\")\\n                time.sleep(10)\\n        \\n    def cleanup(self):\\n        for d in self.deployments:\\n            try:\\n                self.client.delete(d.id)\\n            except:\\n                pass\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    from deploy_citadel import deploy_citadel\\n    \\n    # Prepare verification data (SPoT)\\n                verification_data = {\\n                    \\\"initial_weights\\\": initial_weights,\\n                    \\\"data_shard\\\": (data, target),\\n                    \\\"h_steps\\\": 1,\\n                    \\\"lr\\\": 1e-4,\\n                    \\\"seed\\\": seed\\n                }\\n\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update, verification_data=verification_data)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    \\n                    # Synchronize and adjust density\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights, network_latency=sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        #              resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on Convert tensors to lists for JSON\\n        serializable_update = {\\n            \\\"worker_id\\\": WORKER_ID,\\n            \\\"version\\\": self.version,\\n            \\\"bits\\\": [u['bits'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"indices\\\": [u['indices'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"scales\\\": [u['scale'].item() if u else 0.0 for u in update['updates']]\\n        }\\n        \\n        if verification_data:\\n            serializable_update[\\\"verification_data\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"initial_weights\\\": [w.tolist() for w in verification_data['initial_weights']],\\n                \\\"data_shard\\\": [verification_data['data_shard'][0].tolist(), verification_data['data_shard'][1].tolist()],\\n                \\\"h_steps\\\": verification_data['h_steps'],\\n                \\\"lr\\\": verification_data['lr'],\\n                \\\"seed\\\": verification_data['seed']\\n            }\\n        \\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.post(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/push_update\\\", json=serializable_update, timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on sync time\\n                    self.optimizer.adjust_density(sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # Use Basilica SDK to deploy\\n            max_retries = 3\\n            for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n                try:\\n                    deployment = self.client.deploy(\\n                        name=name,\\n                        source=worker_code,\\n                        gpu_count=1,\\n                        gpu_models=[\\\"A10G\\\"],\\n                        pip_packages=[\\\"torch\\\", \\\"numpy\\\", \\\"basilica-sdk\\\", \\\"requests\\\", \\\"uv\\\"],\\n                        env_vars={\\n                            \\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\": API_KEY,\\n                            \\\"CITADEL_URL\\\": self.config.get(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\", \\\"http://localhost:8000\\\"),\\n                            \\\"WORKER_ID\\\": name,\\n                            \\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\": str(is_compressed),\\n                            \\\"SPARSE_DENSITY\\\": \\\"0.03\\\" # Convert tensors to lists for JSON\\n        serializable_update = {\\n            \\\"worker_id\\\": WORKER_ID,\\n            \\\"version\\\": self.version,\\n            \\\"bits\\\": [u['bits'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"indices\\\": [u['indices'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"scales\\\": [u['scale'].item() if u else 0.0 for u in update['updates']]\\n        }\\n        \\n        if verification_data:\\n            serializable_update[\\\"verification_data\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"initial_weights\\\": [w.tolist() for w in verification_data['initial_weights']],\\n                \\\"data_shard\\\": [verification_data['data_shard'][0].tolist(), verification_data['data_shard'][1].tolist()],\\n                \\\"h_steps\\\": verification_data['h_steps'],\\n                \\\"lr\\\": verification_data['lr'],\\n                \\\"seed\\\": verification_data['seed']\\n            }\\n        \\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.post(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/push_update\\\", json=serializable_update, timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in decentralized setting\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            start_time = time.time()\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on sync time\\n                    self.optimizer.adjust_density(sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # Launch workers\\n        self.launch_workers(num_workers=12) # Scaled to 12 heterogeneous workers\\n        \\n        # 3. Monitor and Aggregate (The Citadel/Aggregator logic)\\n        print(f\\\"Experiment running with Citadel at {citadel_url}. Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            # For SPoT, we need to capture the state before local steps\\n            initial_weights = [p.data.clone().detach() for p in self.model.parameters()]\\n            seed = int(time.time() * 1000) % 100000\\n            torch.manual_seed(seed)\\n\\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # Synchronize and adjust density\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights, network_latency=sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in decentralized setting\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            start_time = time.time()\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on sync time\\n                    self.optimizer.adjust_density(sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # Use Basilica SDK to deploy\\n            max_retries = 3\\n            for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n                try:\\n                    deployment = self.client.deploy(\\n                        name=name,\\n                        source=worker_code,\\n                        gpu_count=1,\\n                        gpu_models=[\\\"A10G\\\"],\\n                        pip_packages=[\\\"torch\\\", \\\"numpy\\\", \\\"basilica-sdk\\\", \\\"requests\\\", \\\"uv\\\"],\\n                        env_vars={\\n                            \\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\": API_KEY,\\n                            \\\"CITADEL_URL\\\": self.config.get(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\", \\\"http://localhost:8000\\\"),\\n                            \\\"WORKER_ID\\\": name,\\n                            \\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\": str(is_compressed),\\n                            \\\"SPARSE_DENSITY\\\": \\\"0.03\\\" # Initial density\\n                        }\\n                    )\\n                    self.deployments.append(deployment)\\n                    print(f\\\"Worker {i} live at: {deployment.url}\\\")\\n                    break\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Attempt {attempt + 1} failed for {name}: {e}\\\")\\n                    if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                        print(f\\\"Failed to deploy {name} after {max_retries} attempts.\\\")\\n                    else:\\n                        time.sleep(5)\\n\\n    def _generate_worker_code(self, is_compressed: bool):\\n        # Read the required files to bundle them\\n        with open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            basilica_training_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            sparseloco_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            main_model_code = f.read()\\n\\n        return f\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nimport os\\nimport sys\\n\\n# Write bundled modules to disk so they can be imported\\nwith open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(basilica_training_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(sparseloco_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(main_model_code)})\\n\\nimport torch\\nimport torch.nn as nn\\nfrom basilica_training import HeterogeneousSparseLoCo\\nimport requests\\nimport json\\nimport time\\n\\n# Config from environment\\nAPI_KEY = os.getenv(\\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\")\\nCITADEL_URL = os.getenv(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\")\\nWORKER_ID = os.getenv(\\\"WORKER_ID\\\")\\nIS_COMPRESSED = os.getenv(\\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\") == \\\"True\\\"\\nSPARSE_DENSITY = float(os.getenv(\\\"SPARSE_DENSITY\\\", \\\"0.03\\\"))\\n\\nclass Worker:\\n    def __init__(self, model_class, is_compressed, density):\\n        self.model = model_class()\\n        self.optimizer = HeterogeneousSparseLoCo(\\n            self.model, \\n            is_compressed=is_compressed,\\n            density=density\\n        )\\n        self.version = 0\\n\\n    def push_update(self, update, verification_data=None):\\n        # Convert tensors to lists for JSON\\n        serializable_update = {\\n            \\\"worker_id\\\": WORKER_ID,\\n            \\\"version\\\": self.version,\\n            \\\"bits\\\": [u['bits'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"indices\\\": [u['indices'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"scales\\\": [u['scale'].item() if u else 0.0 for u in update['updates']]\\n        }\\n        \\n        if verification_data:\\n            serializable_update[\\\"verification_data\\\"] = {\\n                \\\"initial_weights\\\": [w.tolist() for w in verification_data['initial_weights']],\\n                \\\"data_shard\\\": [verification_data['data_shard'][0].tolist(), verification_data['data_shard'][1].tolist()],\\n                \\\"h_steps\\\": verification_data['h_steps'],\\n                \\\"lr\\\": verification_data['lr'],\\n                \\\"seed\\\": verification_data['seed']\\n            }\\n        \\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.post(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/push_update\\\", json=serializable_update, timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in decentralized setting\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            start_time = time.time()\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Dynamic density adjustment based on sync time\\n                    self.optimizer.adjust_density(sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {WORKER_ID} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        # Use a smaller LR for stability in decentralized setting\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            start_time = time.time()\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            # For SPoT, we need to capture the state before local steps\\n            initial_weights = [p.data.clone().detach() for p in self.model.parameters()]\\n            seed = int(time.time() * 1000) % 100000\\n            torch.manual_seed(seed)\\n\\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            step_time = time.time() - start_time\\n            \\n            if self.version % 10 == 0: # More frequent updates for testing\\n                print(f\\\"Step {self.version}, Loss: {loss.item():.4f}, Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                \\n                # Prepare verification data (SPoT)\\n                verification_data = {\\n                    \\\"initial_weights\\\": initial_weights,\\n                    \\\"data_shard\\\": (data, target),\\n                    \\\"h_steps\\\": 1,\\n                    \\\"lr\\\": 1e-4,\\n                    \\\"seed\\\": seed\\n                }\\n\\n                try:\\n                    sync_start = time.time()\\n                    self.push_update(update, verification_data=verification_data)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    sync_time = time.time() - sync_start\\n                    \\n                    # Synchronize and adjust density\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights, network_latency=sync_time)\\n                    \\n                    print(f\\\"Sync successful in {sync_time:.4f}s | Step Time: {step_time:.4f}s\\\")\\n                    \\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-4)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {e}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED, SPARSE_DENSITY)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # handles embedding drift via projection back to subspace.\\n    - Performance: Activation compression composes with SparseLoCo at modest cost; bias reduction from uncompressed replicas anchors aggregation.\\n- **Basilica Analysis:**\\n    - Provides on-demand compute (GPUs/CPUs) via Bittensor (The Bourse) or Datacenters (The Citadel).\\n    - Supports containerized deployments and raw SSH rentals.\\n    - Pay-per-minute via TAO credits.\\n    - Ideal for heterogeneous, permissionless compute environments.\\n\\n## Proposed Strategy: \\\"Heterogeneous Basilica SparseLoCo\\\"\\nTo make decentralized training real on Basilica, we adapt SparseLoCo for its specific constraints:\\n1. **Heterogeneous Compression:** Use Subspace Projection (arXiv:2601.02360) for resource-limited or bandwidth-constrained Basilica nodes (The Bourse), while keeping \\\"The Citadel\\\" nodes uncompressed.\\n2. **Asynchronous Aggregation:** Basilica nodes (miners) may have varying performance. We use an asynchronous version of SparseLoCo where the \\\"Parameter Server\\\" or \\\"Aggregator\\\" handles stale sparse updates with staleness compensation.\\n3. **Verification of Work (SPoT):** Since Basilica miners are self-interested, we use Sparse Proof of Training (SPoT) to verify that the sparse pseudo-gradients are actually computed from the data using deterministic replay.\\n4. **Dynamic Topology & Density:** Nodes may join/leave. If network congestion increases (latency > 2s), the `SparseLoCo` density is automatically reduced (down to 0.1%) to minimize payload size. Conversely, it increases density when bandwidth allows, optimizing for convergence speed.\\n\\n2.  **Adaptive SPoT Verification (`sparseloco.py`)**:\\n    The `BasilicaAggregator` now uses adaptive layer subsampling for **Sparse Proof of Training (SPoT)**. Density -> {self.density:.4f}\\\")\\n        elif network_latency < 0.5:\\n            self.density = min(0.1, self.density * 1.1)\\n            print(f\\\"[DynamicDensity] Low latency ({network_latency:.2f}s). Do not edit GOAL.md.\\n\\n## Goal\\n\\ncome up with state of the art decentralised training algorithms  starting from here https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.15706 . use basilica for compute https://docs.basilica.ai/introduction\\n\\n## State\\n\\n# Quentin - Decentralized Training State\\n\\n## Research & Analysis\\n- **Paper Analysis (SparseLoCo - arXiv:2508.15706):**\\n    - Key Innovation: Combines **DiLoCo** (infrequent communication) with **TOP-k sparsification** and **Error Feedback (EF)**.\\n    - Observation: Outer momentum can be approximated locally by an EF accumulator.\\n    - Performance: Reaches 1-3% sparsity while outperforming full-precision DiLoCo.\\n    - Mechanism: Replaces global outer momentum with a single local EF accumulator.\\n    - Scalability: Shown to scale to 2B parameters and 32+ workers.\\n- **Paper Analysis (Heterogeneous Low-Bandwidth Pre-Training - arXiv:2601.02360):**\\n    - Key Innovation: Combines **SparseLoCo** with **Subspace Projection** for activation/gradient compression.\\n    - Observation: Heterogeneous configurations (mixing compressed and uncompressed replicas) consistently outperform uniform compression.\\n    - Mechanism: Selective compression based on interconnect bandwidth; handles embedding drift via projection back to subspace.\\n    - Performance: Activation compression composes with SparseLoCo at modest cost; bias reduction from uncompressed replicas anchors aggregation.\\n- **Basilica Analysis:**\\n    - Provides on-demand compute (GPUs/CPUs) via Bittensor (The Bourse) or Datacenters (The Citadel).\\n    - Supports containerized deployments and raw SSH rentals.\\n    - Pay-per-minute via TAO credits.\\n    - Ideal for heterogeneous, permissionless compute environments.\\n\\n## Proposed Strategy: \\\"Heterogeneous Basilica SparseLoCo\\\"\\nTo make decentralized training real on Basilica, we adapt SparseLoCo for its specific constraints:\\n1. **Heterogeneous Compression:** Use Subspace Projection (arXiv:2601.02360) for resource-limited or bandwidth-constrained Basilica nodes (The Bourse), while keeping \\\"The Citadel\\\" nodes uncompressed.\\n2. **Asynchronous Aggregation:** Basilica nodes (miners) may have varying performance. We use an asynchronous version of SparseLoCo where the \\\"Parameter Server\\\" or \\\"Aggregator\\\" handles stale sparse updates with staleness compensation.\\n3. **Verification of Work (SPoT):** Since Basilica miners are self-interested, we use Sparse Proof of Training (SPoT) to verify that the sparse pseudo-gradients are actually computed from the data using deterministic replay.\\n4. **Dynamic Topology & Density:** Nodes may join/leave. The algorithm handles a dynamic $R$ (number of replicas) via the asynchronous aggregator. We also implement **Dynamic Density Adjustment** to scale communication overhead based on real-time network latency.\\n\\n## Implementation Progress\\n- [x] Initial project structure defined.\\n- [x] np\\n\\n# Basilica API Key from environment\\nAPI_KEY = os.getenv(\\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\")\\n\\nclass BasilicaTrainer:\\n    def __init__(self, model_class, config):\\n        self.client =     \\n        for i in range(num_workers):\\n            is_compressed = i < num_compressed\\n            name = f\\\"worker-{i}-{'compressed' if is_compressed else 'full'}\\\"\\n            \\n                source=worker_code,\\n                        gpu_count=1,\\n                        gpu_models=[\\\"A10G\\\"],\\n                        pip_packages=[\\\"torch\\\", \\\"numpy\\\", def _generate_worker_code(self, is_compressed: bool):\\n        # Read the required files to bundle them\\n        with open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            basilica_training_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            sparseloco_code = f.read()\\n        with open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            main_model_code = f.read()\\n\\n        return f\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nimport os\\nimport sys\\n\\n# Write bundled modules to disk so they can be imported\\nwith open(\\\"basilica_training.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(basilica_training_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"sparseloco.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(sparseloco_code)})\\nwith open(\\\"main_model.py\\\", \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n    f.write({repr(main_model_code)})\\n\\nimport torch\\nimport torch.nn as nn\\nfrom basilica_training import HeterogeneousSparseLoCo\\nimport requests\\nimport json\\nimport time\\n\\n# Config from environment\\nAPI_KEY = os.getenv(\\\"BASILICA_API_TOKEN\\\")\\nCITADEL_URL = os.getenv(\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\")\\nWORKER_ID = os.getenv(\\\"WORKER_ID\\\")\\nIS_COMPRESSED = os.getenv(\\\"IS_COMPRESSED\\\") == \\\"True\\\"\\n\\nclass Worker:\\n    def __init__(self, model_class, is_compressed):\\n        self.model = model_class()\\n        self.optimizer = HeterogeneousSparseLoCo(\\n            self.model, \\n            is_compressed=is_compressed,\\n            density=0.03\\n        )\\n        self.version = 0\\n\\n    def push_update(self, update):\\n        # Convert tensors to lists for JSON\\n        serializable_update = {{\\n            \\\"worker_id\\\": WORKER_ID,\\n            \\\"version\\\": self.version,\\n            \\\"bits\\\": [u['bits'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"indices\\\": [u['indices'].tolist() if u else [] for u in update['updates']],\\n            \\\"scales\\\": [u['scale'].item() if u else 0.0 for u in update['updates']]\\n        }}\\n        \\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.post(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/push_update\\\", json=serializable_update, timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                return resp.json()\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Push update attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def pull_weights(self):\\n        max_retries = 10\\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\\n            try:\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{{CITADEL_URL}}/weights\\\", timeout=60)\\n                resp.raise_for_status()\\n                data = resp.json()\\n                weights = [torch.tensor(w) for w in data['weights']]\\n                return weights\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Pull weights attempt {{attempt + 1}} failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n                if attempt == max_retries - 1:\\n                    raise\\n                # Exponential backoff with jitter\\n                wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + (torch.rand(1).item() * 2)\\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\\n\\n    def train(self):\\n        print(f\\\"Worker {{WORKER_ID}} starting training loop...\\\")\\n        criterion = nn.MSELoss()\\n        local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)\\n        \\n        while True:\\n            # Generate synthetic data for experiment\\n            data = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            target = torch.randn(16, 128)\\n            \\n            self.model.train()\\n            local_opt.zero_grad()\\n            output = self.model(data)\\n            loss = criterion(output, target)\\n            loss.backward()\\n            local_opt.step()\\n            \\n            if self.version % 100 == 0:\\n                print(f\\\"Step {{self.version}}, Loss: {{loss.item():.4f}}\\\")\\n                update = self.optimizer.get_sparse_update()\\n                try:\\n                    self.push_update(update)\\n                    global_weights = self.pull_weights()\\n                    self.optimizer.synchronize(global_weights)\\n                    # Reset local optimizer for new weights\\n                    local_opt = torch.optim.SGD(self.model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"Sync failed: {{e}}\\\")\\n            \\n            self.version += 1\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    worker = Worker(GlobalModel, IS_COMPRESSED)\\n    worker.train()\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def run_experiment(self, citadel_url: str):\\n        # 1. Update config with live Citadel URL\\n        self.config[\\\"CITADEL_URL\\\"] = citadel_url\\n        \\n        # 2. Launch workers\\n        self.launch_workers(num_workers=2) # Start small\\n        \\n        # 3. Monitor and Aggregate (The Citadel/Aggregator logic)\\n        print(f\\\"Experiment running with Citadel at {citadel_url}. Monitoring workers...\\\")\\n        while True:\\n            try:\\n                headers = {\\\"Authorization\\\": f\\\"Bearer {API_KEY}\\\"}\\n                resp = requests.get(f\\\"{self.config['CITADEL_URL']}/status\\\", headers=headers)\\n                if resp.status_code == 200:\\n                    print(f\\\"Citadel Status: {resp.json()}\\\")\\n                else:\\n                    print(f\\\"Citadel Status Error: {resp.status_code} - {resp.text}\\\")\\n                time.sleep(30)\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"Monitoring error: {e}\\\")\\n                time.sleep(10)\\n        \\n    def cleanup(self):\\n        for d in self.deployments:\\n            try:\\n                self.client.delete(d.id)\\n            except:\\n                pass\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    from main_model import GlobalModel\\n    from deploy_citadel import deploy_citadel\\n Quentin - Decentralized Training State\\n\\n## Research & Analysis\\n- **Paper Analysis (SparseLoCo - arXiv:2508.15706):**\\n    - Key Innovation: Combines **DiLoCo** (infrequent communication) with **TOP-k sparsification** and **Error Feedback (EF)**.\\n    - Observation: Outer momentum can be approximated locally by an EF accumulator.\\n    - Performance: Reaches 1-3% sparsity while outperforming full-precision DiLoCo.\\n    - Mechanism: Replaces global outer momentum with a single local EF accumulator.\\n    - Scalability: Shown to scale to 2B parameters and 32+ workers.\\n- **Paper Analysis (Heterogeneous Low-Bandwidth Pre-Training - arXiv:2601.02360):**\\n    - Key Innovation: Combines **SparseLoCo** with **Subspace Projection** for activation/gradient compression.\\n    - Observation: Heterogeneous configurations (mixing compressed and uncompressed replicas) consistently outperform uniform compression.\\n    - Mechanism: Selective compression based on interconnect bandwidth; handles embedding drift via projection back to subspace.\\n    - Performance: Activation compression composes with SparseLoCo at modest cost; bias reduction from uncompressed replicas anchors aggregation.\\n- **Basilica Analysis:**\\n    - Provides on-demand compute (GPUs/CPUs) via Bittensor (The Bourse) or Datacenters (The Citadel).\\n    - Supports containerized deployments and raw SSH rentals.\\n    - Pay-per-minute via TAO credits.\\n    - Ideal for heterogeneous, permissionless compute environments.\\n\\n## Proposed Strategy: \\\"Heterogeneous Basilica SparseLoCo\\\"\\nTo make decentralized training real on Basilica, we adapt SparseLoCo for its specific constraints:\\n1. **Heterogeneous Compression:** Use Subspace Projection (arXiv:2601.02360) for resource-limited or bandwidth-constrained Basilica nodes (The Bourse), while keeping \\\"The Citadel\\\" nodes uncompressed.\\n2. **Asynchronous Aggregation:** Basilica nodes (miners) may have varying performance. We use an asynchronous version of SparseLoCo where the \\\"Parameter Server\\\" or \\\"Aggregator\\\" handles stale sparse updates with staleness compensation.\\n3. **Verification of Work (SPoT):** Since Basilica miners are self-interested, we use Sparse Proof of Training (SPoT) to verify that the sparse pseudo-gradients are actually computed from the data using deterministic replay.\\n4. **Dynamic Topology:** Nodes may join/leave. The algorithm handles a dynamic $R$ (number of replicas) via the asynchronous aggregator.\\n\\n## Implementation Progress\\n- [x] Initial project structure defined.\\n- [x] Implement core SparseLoCo
